name: Jalance Route
name_original: La ruta de Jalance — circular desde Jalance
description: This difficult 19.8-mile circular mountain biking route from Jalance offers challenging terrain, pine forests, and valley viewpoints.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Jalance are characterized by diverse and rugged terrain, making it a notable destination for outdoor activities. The region features challenging topography with hills and plateaus ranging from 600 to 900 meters, offering significant climbs and descents. The Júcar River carves dramatic canyons, creating technical sections and scenic backdrops for riders. Pine forests and varied landscapes provide a mix of flowy sections and technically demanding single tracks. Best mountain bike trails around Jalance The most popular mountain bike trail is Cofrentes and Cortes de Pallás MTB Loop, a 43.3 miles (69.6 km) trail that takes about 6 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates the challenging terrain between two historic towns. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Júcar Canyons Trail, a moderate 20.4 miles (32.8 km) path. This route offers impressive views as it follows the dramatic river canyons. Local mountain bikers also love the Jalance Route, a 19.8 miles (31.9 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 11 minutes. Mountain biking around Jalance is defined by challenging topography, dramatic river canyons, and extensive pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult, technical descents. The routes in Jalance are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Jalance's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Jalance?
A: Jalance offers a wide variety of mountain biking experiences, with over 30 trails available. These routes cater to different skill levels, ranging from moderate paths to difficult, technical descents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Jalance?
A: The terrain in Jalance is diverse and rugged, characterized by challenging topography with hills and plateaus between 600 and 900 meters. You'll encounter significant climbs and descents, dramatic river canyons carved by the Júcar River, and extensive pine forests. Trails often feature a mix of flowy sections, technical descents, and natural singletracks with roots and small kickers.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Jalance?
A: While many trails in Jalance offer significant challenges, there are a few easier options. Out of the over 30 available routes, 2 are classified as easy, and 19 are moderate, providing good choices for those looking for less demanding rides or to build their skills.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Jalance?
A: Absolutely. Jalance is known for its challenging topography, making it ideal for experienced riders. There are 13 difficult routes available, featuring substantial elevation changes and technical sections. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e910755316/cofrentes-and-cortes-de-pallas-mtb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes and Cortes de Pallás MTB Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 43.3-mile trail with over 1700 meters of elevation gain, navigating challenging terrain between historic towns.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Jalance?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Jalance are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Musica Fountain – Fuente Bella Recreation Area loop from Jalance&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.2-mile circular route, offering a comprehensive tour of the area's natural beauty.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I explore while mountain biking in Jalance?
A: Jalance's trails often pass by impressive natural features. You can encounter the dramatic canyons of the Júcar River, explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5367776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Buitre&lt;/a&gt;, or witness the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4963542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Cabriel and Júcar rivers&lt;/a&gt;. The region's varied elevation also provides numerous viewpoints over the surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there any waterfalls I can see along mountain bike trails in Jalance?
A: Yes, the region around Jalance is home to beautiful waterfalls. You might be able to visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, known for its crystal-clear waters and recreational area, or the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3216823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo de Cortes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are both incredible spots to relax and enjoy the natural environment.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the area around Jalance is rich in history. You can find several castles, such as the important &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2751423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chirel Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers incredible views of the Muela de Cortes and the Júcar River. Another notable site is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4578187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agrás Volcano – Chirel Castle loop from Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt;, specifically incorporate these historical landmarks.

Q: Is parking available near the mountain bike trails in Jalance?
A: While specific parking areas for each trail are not detailed, Jalance is a small municipality, and parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of the town, providing access points to the various trail networks. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy about the trails in Jalance?
A: The mountain bike trails in Jalance are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from numerous reviews. Riders often praise the diverse and rugged terrain, the dramatic river canyons, and the extensive pine forests that make for an engaging and natural riding experience. The mix of flowy sections and technical descents is also frequently highlighted.

Q: What is the best time to visit Jalance for mountain biking?
A: Jalance offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. However, the spring and autumn months typically provide the most pleasant temperatures for riding, avoiding the intense heat of summer. The region's varied terrain means trails can be enjoyed even in cooler weather, though conditions can vary with elevation.

Q: Are there any caves to explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region features several interesting caves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4593119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejador Cave&lt;/a&gt;, located near the Cortes stream, is known for its picturesque and quiet setting. Another natural attraction is the Cueva de Don Juan, found in the abrupt area formed by the Júcar gorge, offering additional exploration opportunities off the bike.
tips: It tells a Moorish story of the Ayora-Cofrentes Valley. The ceramic panel is number 15 of the Los Moriscos route.
Viewpoint with very nice views of the Ayora-Cofrentes Valley. It is located in a parking lot a few meters from the town and the Jalance castle.

faqs.question: Where does La ruta de Jalance mountain bike route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Jalance itself. While specific parking details aren't provided, as a circular route from the town, you can typically find parking within Jalance or at designated areas near the town's outskirts. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse terrain. It typically features sandy sections winding through pine forests, but also lush vegetation in shaded 'umbrias'. The route alternates between well-defined singletrack paths ('sendas de buen cajeado') and older bridle paths, especially closer to Jalance. Be prepared for broken terrain, loose and rocky sections, technical steps, and tight, narrow turns, with some areas potentially showing signs of deterioration. Forest tracks are also common.

faqs.question: How difficult is La ruta de Jalance mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as difficult. It demands very good physical condition and significant mountain biking experience. You should be prepared for sections that may require pushing your bike. Some parts feature exposure due to their position on steeply inclined slopes, and there can be very technical climbs.

faqs.question: What points of interest or viewpoints will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable spots. You'll pass by the Fuente del Medico and enjoy a Viewpoint over the Ayora-Cofrentes Valley. Additionally, there are facilities like the Tollo Pillete Recreational and Camping Area and the Fuente del Tobarro Recreation Area.

faqs.question: Is La ruta de Jalance dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the region are generally accessible, specific regulations for dogs on this particular mountain bike route are not detailed. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other trail users, and to check local park or natural area rules if you encounter any protected zones.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Jalance area can be enjoyed for mountain biking across several seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winters are typically mild, but check weather conditions for rain, which can make sandy and loose sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that specific permits or entrance fees are required to mountain bike La ruta de Jalance. However, always respect private property and any local regulations you may encounter, especially if passing through agricultural or protected areas.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll ride along parts of 'La Ruta de Jalance' (54% of the route), 'Jarafuel, Muela de Juey y el Castillico' (13%), 'Los Castillos del Valle' (13%), 'Los Cañones del Júcar' (5%), and 'Senda los Burros' (6%).

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or services available in Jalance for cyclists?
faqs.answer: Jalance, as the starting and ending point of this circular route, is likely to offer basic services such as cafes, pubs, and potentially accommodation. The route itself passes by recreational areas like Tollo Pillete and Fuente del Tobarro, which may offer facilities.

faqs.question: What should I bring for mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' grading and varied terrain, you should bring appropriate mountain biking gear, including a helmet, gloves, and protective eyewear. Carry plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit, and a first-aid kit. Due to potentially technical sections and exposure, consider bringing a GPS device or a fully charged phone with the Komoot app for navigation.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while riding?
faqs.answer: You'll experience scenic views dominated by extensive pine forests and the rich plant life of shaded 'umbrias'. The region is part of the Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes, offering vistas of the Júcar and Jarafuel rivers, and potentially impressive views of canyons like the Cañones del Júcar. The landscape is characterized by hills and plateaus, providing a beautiful and extensive network of mountain biking routes.
